recommendations: add and run a focused MCP test suite (smoke + contract + error-path) for all exposed endpoints since current test status is empty
add CI workflows in `mcp_output` (lint/type/test on PR and nightly integration against upstream astropy main)
pin and constrain dependencies in `mcp_output/requirements.txt` (including astropy/numpy compatibility ranges) and add a reproducible lock file
harden import-time behavior in `adapter.py` with lazy imports and clear fallback/error messages for optional deps (scipy/matplotlib/pandas/pyarrow/h5py/fsspec/dask)
introduce strict input/output Pydantic schemas per endpoint (units
coordinates
FITS args) with consistent error normalization
add security guardrails for file-based endpoints (`open/getdata/getheader/writeto/append/update`) such as path allowlists
size limits
and overwrite controls
add performance benchmarks and caching for expensive operations (WCS transforms
large FITS I/O
sigma clipping) plus regression thresholds in CI
improve observability with structured logging
request IDs
and endpoint-level timing/failure metrics
document endpoint coverage and limitations in `README_MCP.md` (supported Astropy versions
optional feature flags
known edge cases)
add backward-compatibility/versioning policy for MCP endpoint names and payloads
expand resilience tests for large/invalid/corrupt FITS and malformed coordinate/unit strings
reduce intrusiveness by wrapping high-level stable APIs only and avoiding private `_src` cosmology paths where possible
add example client scripts and golden-response fixtures for key workflows (FITS inspect
SkyCoord transform
Table I/O
WCS pixel↔world)
enable static quality gates in plugin code (`ruff`
`mypy`/`pyright`
`bandit`) aligned with upstream standards
add graceful degradation matrix tests for environments with missing optional dependencies to verify predictable endpoint availability

success_factors: Workflow status is successful end-to-end with all planned nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter strategy is appropriate for Astropy and aligns with high import feasibility (0.9).
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio and exposed a broad endpoint set (22 endpoints in service info).
Repository analysis was comprehensive (large codebase recognized, 1060 files discovered; 21 processed in generation workflow scope).
No runtime errors or warnings were recorded during workflow execution.
failure_reasons: Quality validation is incomplete: original project tests are marked failed/empty and MCP test details are effectively absent, so functional correctness is not yet proven.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external contextual confidence for architecture/documentation enrichment.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; repository ingestion used zip fallback, which is resilient but may be slower and less metadata-rich than git-native checkout.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully with deep structural scan and module identification; good coverage for endpoint candidate discovery.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully and produced all expected scaffold files, adapter, service entrypoint, and README.
node_performance.test_time: Service health check passed, but substantive endpoint contract/smoke/error-path testing is missing; this is the main execution-quality gap.
